title: Update working memory from PR 356 auth setup session
summary: Durable auth setup routing, setup-required page, and E2E context learned during session ffe6a727.

claims.text: When credentials are missing and check_auth returns SetupRequired, auth_gate keeps API and WebSocket requests at 401 AUTH_SETUP_REQUIRED, passes local page requests and /onboarding or /onboarding/ page requests through with loopback identity, and redirects other page requests to /setup-required.

/setup-required still renders the setup-required template before credentials exist, but setup_required_handler redirects to /login once the credential store reports setup complete so stale bookmarks do not show Authentication Not Configured.

The session kept remote /onboarding reachable during setup because the onboarding page step 0 requires a setup code; blocking it at the auth gate broke the forced-auth onboarding flow, while other remote page paths should still land on /setup-required.

The session kept /setup-required as an auth public path to avoid setup redirect loops, but added the handler guard so the public route cannot display stale setup-required content after auth is configured.

For PR 356, review comments that proposed fixes around auth_setup_pending or authOnly were rejected because those symbols are not part of the onboarding implementation; the onboarding view derives its auth step from /api/auth/status and auth?.setup_required.

The logout button updates after runtime auth status change E2E test may be flaky or have a separate runtime-auth-status bug: local validation for PR 356 observed #logoutBtn present but hidden after dispatching moltis:auth-status-changed, and this session did not fix it.

sources.title: SWE-chat Claude Code session ffe6a727: Improve coverage from PR checks

metadata.reason: The session changed the initial remote redirect fix after the onboarding-auth E2E failed on /setup-required and concluded that remote /onboarding must remain reachable for the setup-code auth flow.
The session accepted the Greptile concern that stale /setup-required content was misleading after setup while preserving the public route to prevent setup-time redirect loops.
The session reviewed the PR comments and explicitly rejected the auth_setup_pending/authOnly suggestions as hallucinated against the actual onboarding implementation.
The session transcript includes a local E2E failure for the runtime auth-status logout button test and records that it was treated as unrelated and left unresolved.
